currentState: Chapter 23 defines the relationship through patricide and failed succession ritual. Father and son meet in the Imperial Chamber only long enough for Arthas to turn filial address into murder.
powerDynamic: Terenas holds ceremonial authority and none of the room’s practical power. Arthas enters carrying Frostmourne’s force and uses that force to erase the father before any ordinary royal or familial order can reassert itself.

physicalDynamics.pattern: The chapter’s defining image is interrupted rising: Terenas half out of the throne while Arthas closes the distance with the inevitability of a verdict already made elsewhere.

nameUsage.arthasCallsTerenas: Father, immediately before the strike, making the intimacy of the address part of the horror rather than a restraint on it.
nameUsage.terenasCallsArthas: Not meaningfully recoverable on page beyond the older voice attempting to answer the prince before the blow lands.

history.event: Arthas murders Terenas in the Imperial Chamber before Jaina and Sylvanas can cross the room to intervene.

trajectory: Royal father and son -> chapter 23’s public patricide.

stateHistory.currentState: Chapter 23 defines the relationship through patricide and failed succession ritual. Father and son meet in the Imperial Chamber only long enough for Arthas to turn filial address into murder.
